body:
While integrating `agent-commander@0.6.0` into `link-assistant/hive-mind` for issue https://github.com/link-assistant/hive-mind/issues/1043, basic `qwen` and `gemini` tool launch support is present, but two parity gaps remain for hive-mind's generated solve sessions.

Gaps found:

1. Prompt-file/large-prompt handling is still limited to `claude`, `codex`, `opencode`, and `agent`.
   - `src/index.mjs` has `PROMPT_FILE_TOOLS = new Set(['claude', 'codex', 'opencode', 'agent'])`.
   - `qwen` and `gemini` currently pass the combined prompt inline through CLI args (`-p`), which is risky for hive-mind's large issue/PR prompts.

2. Raw passthrough parity is missing for `qwen` and `gemini`.
   - `claude`, `codex`, `opencode`, and `agent` builders accept `executable`, `extraEnv`, and `extraArgs`.
   - `qwen` and `gemini` builders hardcode `qwen`/`gemini` and ignore those passthrough options.
   - The README also documents `--tool-executable` as applying only to `claude`, `codex`, `opencode`, or `agent`.

Expected behavior:

- `qwen` and `gemini` should either support `promptFile`/temporary prompt files and raw `executable`/`extraEnv`/`extraArgs` passthrough like the other tools, or expose/document these as intentionally unsupported so callers can branch on capability metadata.

Why this matters:

- Hive-mind solve prompts are often large and contain issue details, PR feedback, repository context, and workflow instructions.
- Hive-mind also supports per-tool executable overrides for embedded adapters; the agent-commander path can preserve that for the first four tools but not yet for `qwen`/`gemini`.

Observed with:

- `agent-commander@0.6.0`
- Hive-mind PR: https://github.com/link-assistant/hive-mind/pull/1044
title: Add qwen and gemini prompt-file and passthrough parity
